recommendation_rationale: The Glasgow database contains 14,000+ connections but most are granular lexical observations (individual word senses sharing etymology), not structural metaphors that shape reasoning. The trial slice below identifies 25 connections that encode genuine cross-domain reasoning patterns still alive in modern English. Recommend extracting these as mappings and cataloging the Glasgow database as a reference work.

candidates.name: ARGUMENT IS WAR
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - canonical Lakoff metaphor, massively productive in modern English, already in catalog (skip extraction)
candidates.description: War vocabulary structures how we understand, conduct, and talk about arguments. Already in catalog -- included as calibration.

candidates.name: IDEAS ARE PLANTS
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - deeply productive: 'seminal idea', 'budding theory', 'root cause', 'branch of knowledge', 'fertile imagination'. Structures how we think about intellectual growth.
candidates.description: Plant growth and development structures how we conceptualize the origin, development, and fruiting of ideas.

candidates.name: EMOTIONS ARE WEATHER
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - highly productive: 'stormy relationship', 'sunny disposition', 'cloud of depression', 'thunderous rage'. Encodes emotions as external, uncontrollable forces.
candidates.description: Weather phenomena structure emotional experience -- emotions arrive like weather, are experienced as atmospheric conditions, and pass like storms.

candidates.name: SOCIAL CONFLICT IS WAR
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - deeply structural: 'culture war', 'class warfare', 'battle lines', 'warring factions'. Frames disagreement as existential combat.
candidates.description: Social disagreements and tensions are structured as military conflicts, importing escalation logic, sides, and victory/defeat.

candidates.name: MORALITY IS WAR
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - productive: 'moral crusade', 'defend one's honor', 'indefensible position', 'moral high ground'. Frames ethics as territory to hold.
candidates.description: Moral positions are conceived as fortifications to defend or territory to hold, making ethics adversarial by default.

candidates.name: PSYCHOLOGICAL STATES ARE WARFARE
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - foundational to psychoanalysis and CBT: 'defense mechanism', 'inner conflict', 'reinforcement', 'ego defenses'. War vocabulary shaped psychology as a discipline.
candidates.description: The mind's internal dynamics are structured as military operations -- defenses, conflicts, reinforcements, and strategic retreats.

candidates.name: PROSPERITY IS PLANT GROWTH
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - ancient and still alive: 'flourishing business', 'blooming economy', 'growth sector'. Plant growth is the default metaphor for economic success.
candidates.description: Success and prosperity are understood through plant growth -- things flourish, bloom, bear fruit, or wither and die on the vine.

candidates.name: DIFFICULTY IS WEIGHT
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - deeply embodied: 'heavy workload', 'burden of proof', 'light duties', 'weighty matters'. Physical weight structures how we gauge difficulty.
candidates.description: The felt heaviness of physical objects structures how we conceive of and communicate difficulty, responsibility, and seriousness.

candidates.name: BEAUTY IS A FLOWER
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - ancient, cross-cultural, still productive: 'in bloom', 'a rose by any name', 'flowering of art'. Imports temporality -- beauty blossoms and fades.
candidates.description: Beauty is understood as flowering -- it blooms, blossoms, fades, and wilts, importing the plant life cycle into aesthetic judgment.

candidates.name: HARM IS A THORN
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - productive: 'thorn in one's side', 'thorny issue', 'weeding out', 'barren effort'. The harmful parts of plants structure how we talk about obstacles and nuisances.
candidates.description: The injurious parts of plants -- thorns, weeds, barrenness -- structure how we conceptualize harm, nuisance, and fruitlessness.

candidates.name: LOVE IS WAR
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - productive: 'conquest', 'surrender to love', 'battle of the sexes', 'playing the field'. Love as strategic campaign with winners and losers.
candidates.description: Romantic pursuit is structured as military campaign -- lovers conquer, surrender, lay siege, and wage battles of the heart.

candidates.name: ANIMALS ARE MORAL AGENTS
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - deeply embedded: 'bestial', 'beastly behavior', 'brutal', 'animal instincts'. Animals as the anchor for our moral vocabulary of degradation.
candidates.description: Animal qualities serve as the vocabulary for moral degradation -- to be bad is to be beastly, brutish, or animal.

candidates.name: DEATH IS A JOURNEY
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - universal, cross-cultural: 'he passed away', 'departed', 'last journey', 'crossing over'. Travel structures our primary euphemistic and conceptual framework for death.
candidates.description: Death is conceived as departure on a journey -- the dead pass on, depart, cross over, and go to a better place.

candidates.name: ILLNESS IS AN INVADER
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - load-bearing in medicine: 'fighting cancer', 'the body's defenses', 'viral attack', 'immune system'. Structures medical practice and patient experience.
candidates.description: Disease is understood as military invasion of the body, making treatment a counterattack and the immune system a defense force.

candidates.name: MENTAL HEALTH IS PHYSICAL HEALTH
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - foundational: 'sound mind', 'mental health', 'sick thoughts', 'healthy attitude'. Physical health vocabulary is the entire framework for psychological wellbeing.
candidates.description: Psychological states are understood through the vocabulary of physical health -- minds are sound or sick, thoughts are healthy or diseased.

candidates.name: CLEVERNESS IS QUICKNESS
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - deeply embedded since Old English: 'quick-witted', 'sharp', 'agile mind', 'mental agility'. Speed of physical movement = speed of thought.
candidates.description: Intelligence is conceived as speed of movement -- the clever are quick, agile, nimble; the foolish are slow, plodding, dull.

candidates.name: COURAGE IS STRENGTH
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - ancient and pervasive: 'strong character', 'pillar of strength', 'fortitude', 'stout-hearted'. Physical strength is the default metric for moral courage.
candidates.description: Moral and emotional courage is understood through physical strength -- the brave are strong, the cowardly are weak.

candidates.name: EMOTION IS HEAT
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - universal embodied metaphor: 'hot-tempered', 'burning passion', 'warm welcome', 'cool reception', 'heated argument'. Temperature = emotional intensity.
candidates.description: Emotional intensity maps onto temperature -- strong emotions are hot, absence of emotion is cold, moderate positive feeling is warm.

candidates.name: FOOD IS INFORMATION
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - productive: 'food for thought', 'digest information', 'half-baked idea', 'appetite for knowledge'. Consuming food = consuming information.
candidates.description: Information and ideas are conceived as food to be consumed, digested, and metabolized -- making comprehension a form of eating.

candidates.name: DECISION IS A PATH
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - fundamental: 'take a step', 'choose a path', 'crossroads', 'at a fork in the road'. Movement through space = progress through decisions.
candidates.description: Decisions are understood as choosing paths -- we take steps, follow courses, reach crossroads, and sometimes lose our way.

candidates.name: LIFE IS A PERFORMANCE
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - Shakespeare's 'All the world's a stage' is the canonical expression. Still alive: 'playing a role', 'stage of life', 'behind the scenes', 'acting out'.
candidates.description: Life events are understood as theatrical performances -- people play roles, enter and exit stages, and perform for audiences.

candidates.name: AUTHORITY IS HEIGHT
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - universal orientational metaphor: 'high office', 'top brass', 'above the law', 'lower ranks'. Vertical space = social hierarchy.
candidates.description: Social and political authority is mapped onto vertical space -- power is up, subordination is down.

candidates.name: TIME IS MOVEMENT
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - one of the deepest metaphors in any language: 'time flies', 'time passes', 'the years crept by'. Movement through space = passage of time.
candidates.description: The passage of time is understood through physical movement -- time flows, flies, crawls, marches on, and stands still.

candidates.name: UNDERSTANDING IS SEEING
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - perhaps the most productive conceptual metaphor in English: 'I see what you mean', 'clear thinking', 'brilliant insight', 'shed light on'.
candidates.description: Intellectual comprehension is structured as visual perception -- to understand is to see, clarity is brightness, confusion is darkness.

candidates.name: TRADE IS SLAUGHTER
candidates.trial_assessment: PASS - interesting fossil: 'make a killing', 'slaughter the competition', 'butcher a deal'. Violence vocabulary in commerce reveals an older conceptual frame.
candidates.description: Commercial success is expressed through killing vocabulary -- traders make a killing, slaughter competition, and butcher deals.
